Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Request Trace Audit model · Theory Request Trace Audit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: One distributed trace contains every authoritative AWS audit fact. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
